https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/r.commit_message:
Bug 1948342 - Update toolbar and tabstrip context menus to include vertical tabs and sidebar r=sidebar-reviewers,jsudiaman

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D239813

description.text:
We want to:

- rename "Customize..." to "Customize Toolbar..." (this should also affect the main menu)
- rename "Auto-Hide" for the downloads button to "Hide Button When Empty"
- hide the pin/remove options and the subsequent separator for flexible spaces (so the menu, when opened there will just show the options to hide/show toolbar(s) and to customize toolbar...)
- reorder the "Only show on New Tab" item in the bookmarks toolbar submenu to be between "always" and "never", instead of at the end.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Flexible space item shows "Pin to Overflow Menu" item in context menu

description.text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:56.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/56.0
Build ID: 20170722072631

Steps to reproduce:

1. Get the overflow menu icon to show up by pinning an item, say the home button, into it.
2. Go into the Customize mode, and add a flexible space onto the nav-bar. Exit Customize mode.
3. Right click this flexible space.


Actual results:

1. There is a "Pin to Overflow Menu" item on the menu, and it is not disabled.
2. Clicking it doesn't remove the flexible space, but the something-was-pinned-to-overflow-menu animation plays out.


Expected results:

The "Pin to Overflow Menu" item should probably be disabled, as per https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1366813#c0 ("you can only add flexible spacers to toolbars, not panels")
